differential_diagnoses: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS) syndrome
Kaposi's varicelliform eruption (superimposed on chronic dermatosis)
next_steps: Flow cytometry on peripheral blood to evaluate for Sézary cells
T-cell receptor gene rearrangement studies on skin biopsy and blood
Immunohistochemical staining of skin biopsy for CD3, CD4, CD8, CD7, and CD30
PET/CT scan to better characterize pulmonary nodules and lymphadenopathy
Biopsy of a suspicious pulmonary nodule and/or the most prominent lymph node
Taper systemic glucocorticoids while awaiting definitive diagnosis to avoid masking potential malignancy
Continue targeted antimicrobial therapy for identified Pseudomonas aeruginosa infection

differential_diagnoses: Drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms (DRESS)
next_steps: Excisional lymph node biopsy for histopathology and flow cytometry
CT-guided biopsy of the pulmonary mass
Peripheral blood flow cytometry for Sézary cells
T-cell receptor gene rearrangement studies on peripheral blood and biopsy tissue
Direct immunofluorescence and indirect immunofluorescence testing for bullous dermatoses
Bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage if lung biopsy is not feasible
Interdisciplinary consultation with dermatology, hematology-oncology, and infectious disease
